text:
Two new pyrazole derivatives namely 2-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazole-3-carbonyl)-N-phenylhydrazine-1-carboxamide (**Pyz-1**) and 4-amino-5-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol (**Pyz-2**) were prepared and characterized by means of FT-IR, NMR and MS. Density functional theory at the level of B3LYP/6-311++G** was employed to investigate their chemistry behavior such as molecular electronic features, orbitals and energy gap as well as thermodynamic stability of them in gaseous and aqueous solution phases.

Herein, we assessed the in vitro bioactivity of the compounds, namely their possible applications in diabetes and free radical scavenging. We found that the derivatives showed potent inhibition against α-glucosidase and α-amylase, with Pyz-1 that exhibited $IC_{50}$ values (75.62 and 119.3 µM) near to that of the standard drug Acarbose. The compounds were assayed for their antioxidant activity in various assays like DPPH, ABTS, FRAP and $H_2O_2$ scavenging assays and all exhibited remarkable antioxidant activity. In addition, the compounds were evaluated as xanthine oxidase inhibitors, in which compound Pyz-2 showed $IC_{50}$ value of 10.75 µM and was more potent than that of the standard drug Allopurinol. Molecular docking experiments of compounds with the enzymes confirmed that the compounds have good binding affinity and stable interaction with the enzymes. An integrated analysis of the ADMET properties revealed that both derivatives fulfilled Lipinski’s rules, implying good drug-like properties.
